question:
Will SATS's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21?

Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.

Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/SATS for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used.

Background: EchoStar Corporation provides pay-tv services in the United States, Mexico, Canada, South and Central America, Asia, Africa, Australia, Europe, India, and the Middle East. The Pay-TV segment offers a direct broadcast and fixed satellite, owned and leased satellites, leased fiber optic networks, in-home services, and call center operation services; digital broadcast operations, including satellite uplinking/downlinking, transmission and, other services to third-party pay-TV providers; multichannel, live-linear and on-demand streaming over-the-top Internet-based domestic, international, Latino, and Freestream video programming services; and receiver systems. Its Wireless segment provides wireless communication services and products; and a range of wireless devices. The Broadband and Satellite Services offers broadband satellite technologies, and internet products and services to consumer customers, including home and small to medium-sized businesses; managed services, equipment, hardware, satellite services, and communications solutions to government and enterprise customers, as well as to the unserved and underserved consumer, enterprise, aeronautical, and government markets; and integrated multi-transport solutions that enable airline and airline service providers to deliver in-flight network connectivity. This segment also designs, provides, and installs gateway and terminal equipment to customers for other satellite systems; and designs, develops, constructs, and provides telecommunication networks comprising satellite ground segment systems and terminals to mobile system operators and enterprise customers. Its Other segment consists of 5G network and 5G network deployment operations. The company sells its products and services under the Boost Mobile, DISH, Gen Mobile, Hughes, Hughesnet, and Sling brands. The company was formerly known as EchoStar Holding Corporation. EchoStar Corporation was founded in 1980 and is headquartered in Englewood, Colorado.

evidences.title: Echostar (SATS) Analysis - Vantix Research
evidences.content: ∙ Paid #### Introduction: EchoStar Corporation is a satellite communications and connectivity company that, as of early 2024, merged with DISH Network to form a diversified telecom entity. The combined company operates a broad range of businesses, from satellite broadband and defense communications (Hughes Network Systems), to consumer satellite TV and streaming (DISH TV and Sling TV), to mobile wireless service (Boost Mobile). Until recently, EchoStar was burdened with heavy debt and a precarious financial position. Entering 2025, it had nearly $27B in debt, was burning cash (about -$2B free cash flow annually), and faced an FCC investigation into unused spectrum. Bankruptcy concerns loomed as legacy businesses declined and an ambitious 5G network buildout strained resources. [...] Future Catalysts: Frankly, there are not many growth catalysts for the legacy satellite TV arm, the focus will be on managing its decline profitably. EchoStar could attempt to merge or partner this segment with a similar player (the long rumored DISH/DirecTV merger idea) to squeeze out cost synergies, though now that Dish is fully under EchoStar, any such deal would have to involve AT&T (which co-own...

evidences.content: ### What is EchoStar Corporation's business model? EchoStar Corporation generates revenue through three main segments: Pay-TV, Retail Wireless, and Broadband and Satellite Services. It offers multichannel video programming and streaming services via brands like DISH TV and Sling TV, while providing retail wireless services through Boost Mobile. Additionally, its Hughes division delivers satellite internet and managed network services to a variety of customers, including residential, enterprise, and government sectors. ### What is the highest forecasted price for SATS EchoStar Corporation? evidences.title: EchoStar (SATS) Stock Analysis 2026 – Graphs, Risks, Opportunities & Valuation ✅
evidences.content: holders exposed if the final structure is less favorable than hoped. A second major risk is operating erosion. Echostar is not dealing with balance sheet pressure in isolation. Parts of the core business are still shrinking. In 2025, Dish TV lost about 636,000 net subscribers. Sling TV lost about 167,000 and broadband lost about 144,000. That matters because declining subscribers reduce cash generation exactly when the company needs stability the most. Another key risk is wireless and spectrum execution. Spectrum is one of the big pillars of the equity thesis, but it only has strategic value if Echostar can comply with buildout requirements, defend licenses, or monetize them on attractive terms. The 10K makes clear that the company's wireless spectrum licenses remain subject to interim [...] wireless spectrum licenses remain subject to interim and final buildout and renewal requirements and that recent FCC deadline extensions were conditional. That means regulatory and execution risk are still very real. Then there is impairment and accounting volatility. Echoar reported a massive 2025 comprehensive loss and the filing shows large impairment charges tied to network and internati...

evidences.content: The company's operations are segmented into Pay-TV, Wireless, and Broadband and Satellite Services. While the press release did not break down segment performance for the quarter, the overall results suggest ongoing pressures, potentially from competitive markets in video services and the capital-intensive nature of its wireless and satellite broadband expansions. ### Market Performance and Forward-Looking Estimates Despite the negative earnings reaction, EchoStar's stock has shown resilience over recent weeks. Prior to the report, the share price had posted modest gains: Past Month: +1.8% Past Two Weeks: +4.5% Past Week: +4.7% [...] This suggests that some investors may have been anticipating a turnaround or were focused on broader market trends. However, the post-earnings drop highlights the weight given to the actual quarterly results. Looking ahead, analyst estimates for the coming periods present a sober outlook. For the first quarter of 2026, the consensus points to another loss per share of $1.01 on sales of approximately $3.73 billion. The full-year 2026 view is similarly cautious, with an estimated EPS loss of $3.69 on sales of $14.86 billion. The company did not provid...

The company offers broadband internet products, broadband equipment, hardware products and installation gateway and terminal equipment and communications solutions. Its service offerings include pay TV, satellite network, wireless services, broadband service, communications services and managed services. EchoStar also provides other technologies including geostationary high throughput satellites, low-earth orbit networks, medium-earth orbit systems. It provides services under Boost Mobile, Hughesnet, Hughes, Dish and Sling brand names. The company serves various industries including enterprise, aeronautical, consumer, business and government. evidences.title: EchoStar - Wikipedia
evidences.content: On September 30, 2024, EchoStar announced that it would divest its video distribution unit, Dish Network, to rival DirecTV in a debt exchange transaction that includes a payment of $1 and the assumption of approximately $9.8 billion in debt. The deal also included $2.5 billion of financing from TPG Angelo Gordon and other co-investors to allow EchoStar to refinance the company's November 2024 debt maturity. The deal would've allowed EchoStar to reduce its debt and refocus its resources toward its wireless Boost Mobile and satellite connectivity businesses. The deal was expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2025, contingent on regulatory approval and bondholders writing off nearly $1.6 billion in Dish-related debt. In November, DirecTV called off the deal due to opposition from [...] On August 26, 2025, AT&T announced that it would acquire spectrum licenses from EchoStar for $23 billion, in a cash deal expected to close in mid-2026. On September 8, 2025, SpaceX announced it had struck a $17 billion cash and stock deal to purchase the rights to some of EchoStar's wireless spectrum licenses. Echostar said it believed that the two spectrum deals with AT&T and SpaceX would resol...

evidences.content: | | | --- | | Symbol: | SATSV | | CUSIP: | 278768205 | | First Trade Date: | August 20, 2019 | | Suspension Date: | September 11, 2019 | | Settlement Date: | September 12, 2019 | | | | --- | | Symbol: | DISHV | | CUSIP: | 25470M109 | | First Trade Date: | August 20, 2019 | | Suspension Date: | September 11, 2019 | | Settlement Date: | September 12, 2019 | [...] | | | --- | | Company/Parent: | EchoStar Corporation | | Issue: | Class A Common Stock | | CUSIP: | 278768106 | | Symbol: | SATS | | Record Date: | August 19, 2019 | | Payment Date: | September 10, 2019 | | EX Date: | September 11, 2019 | | Due Bill Redemption Date: | September 12, 2019 | | Ratio of Spin-Off/ Distribution: | One (1) share of EchoStar BSS Corporation for each share of EchoStar Corporation Class A Common Stock held. | An EX distribution when issued market will be established for EchoStar Corporation. Trades in the EX distribution when issued market will not carry due-bills for the spin off/distribution.
